reviewer: build-wave agent (real end-to-end headless run + responsive matrix, revision 1)

rubric.evidence: REAL end-to-end browser run (own headless Chrome, WASM, no-GPU): first visit showed the Download button; after clicking it the shared loader reached ready and clicking Diarize produced a real result on the bundled ted.wav — backend WASM, 164 ms, 2 speakers, 10 segments, timeline rendered (Speaker 1 / Speaker 3 lanes + time axis). Console clean (no errors). Standalone raw-model check on mlk.wav also produced real segments (logits [1,767,7], ids [0,2,3]). conformance: loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass.
rubric.notes: Real ONNX inference confirmed (onnx-community/pyannote-segmentation-3.0, AutoModelForAudioFrameClassification + post_process_speaker_diarization) — not a canned result.

rubric.evidence: Output is the documented diarization shape: per-frame powerset logits [1, frames, 7] decoded into speaker segments {id, start, end, confidence}. Verified live: ted.wav → 10 segments across 2 speaker ids with monotonic timestamps; the See-inside activity strip reflects the per-frame silence/one/overlap classes. Matches the model card's example output shape.

conformance: declares-model-id=pass (onnx-community/pyannote-segmentation-3.0 linked), declares-task=pass (audio-frame-classification), declares-backend=pass (WASM), declares-quantisation=pass (fp32), inference-off-main-thread=pass (Web Worker). At-a-glance matches the model actually loaded.

shared-auto-init-loader=pass. First visit correctly surfaced Download (never a silent large download); after download the run control enabled. The shared createModelLoader handles current/stale/partial/evicted; a per-model clear-cache control is provided by the loader.
rubric.notes: Returning-user auto-init is handled by the shared loader (same contract as other built demos).

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region=pass (role=status aria-live), errors-on-the-page=pass. Audio-decode failures surface a labelled .err status; the run button stays disabled until a model and audio are both ready; no WebGPU required (WASM).
rubric.notes: Offline/eviction handled by the shared loader (partial→Re-download).

rubric.evidence: Every control operable: sample chips, file upload (accessible label), audio player, Diarize button. buttons-have-names=pass. Verified via the real run (Diarize produced output). The wild page adds user-initiated mic recording with a 30s cap and honest unsupported fallback.

The timeline, segment table and talk-time stats are computed from the real per-frame logits + post_process_speaker_diarization in the worker; confirmed by the live ted.wav result (2 speakers, 10 segments). No hardcoded/canned result anywhere.

conformance: buttons-have-names=pass, images-have-alt=pass. Status is role=status aria-live; timeline/segment regions are aria-live; the speaker palette is a WCAG-friendly categorical set that reads in light+dark; the activity canvas carries an aria-label. Applied modern-web-guidance 'accessibility'.
rubric.notes: Colour is paired with an explicit 'Speaker N' text label, not colour-only.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean (no horizontal overflow, no console/network errors, both classes). Timeline lanes use flex with min-inline-size:0 tracks; the segment table scrolls; controls wrap. Applied 'css-layout' (flex 1 1 auto, logical properties).

Inference (164 ms measured) runs off the main thread in a Web Worker with a transferred audio buffer; the main thread only renders DOM/canvas. No blocking loop. conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible loading progress status live region for on-device model
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce load/progress/ready/error in an accessible live region; label every control; keep keyboard operability + visible focus.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live status line, labelled buttons + upload label, aria-live timeline/transcript regions, aria-label on the activity canvas, and text speaker labels alongside colour.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance live-status-region / buttons-have-names = pass; end-to-end run console clean

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel layout no horizontal overflow mobile desktop
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Flexbox with flex:1 1 <basis>, intrinsic sizing, logical properties; wrap to avoid narrow-viewport overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — timeline lanes are flex rows with a min-inline-size:0 track, controls use flex-wrap, tables scroll; logical properties throughout.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean (no horizontal overflow)

guidanceConsulted.query: keep UI responsive INP off main thread worker audio
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run inference and heavy processing off the main thread; transfer buffers rather than clone.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all inference runs in a Web Worker; the 16 kHz Float32Array is transferred (not cloned) to the worker.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared design system).

openQuestions: pyannote segmentation-3.0 caps at 3 concurrent speakers per window and can over-segment single-speaker audio (mlk/ted show apparent speaker changes). Would a bundled genuine 2-speaker, clearly-licensed conversation clip make the demo read better without hotlinking?
Should the multi-model page align wav2vec2 word timings to segment boundaries more tightly (currently midpoint attribution)?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a bundled, clearly-licensed two-speaker conversation sample so the timeline shows a clear back-and-forth by default.
Add an optional clustering step (embedding-based) so speaker ids are stable across the whole file, not just per-window.

summary: Revision-1 build-wave pass for speaker-diarization (audio-frame-classification, onnx-community/pyannote-segmentation-3.0, WASM fp32). Real in-browser inference confirmed end-to-end (ted.wav → 2 speakers, 10 segments, 164 ms, console clean) with a real speaker timeline + activity strip + talk-time analytics; first-visit Download honoured; desktop+mobile responsive clean. Distinct from the built silero-VAD (voice-activity only) — this assigns speaker identities. Support seeded needs-review pending a full agent screenshot matrix.
